body.title: [triage] Background slate writer dirties tree mid-proof; fix or gate writes
body.intention: I intend that `proof_emitter.py` never has to use `parent_ref` gymnastics to work around uncommitted slate changes that appeared between stub+impl commits; the slate-writing mechanism should either batch-write or gate itself behind proof_emitter's clean-tree gate.
body.description:
**Friction during proof-on-close sprints:** when proof_emitter runs and the working tree is dirty (because a background writer touched today's slate.txt between commits), the proof binds to the wrong commit. Workaround: manually specify `parent_ref=<stub_sha>` to point proof_emitter at the stub commit. This saved cycles during T-granny-mru-and-stall-alarm (used `parent_ref` twice). Not blocking, but a pattern that will recur every sprint with multiple proof commits.

**Root:** something (likely the slate sync daemon or a background monitoring process) writes to `devlab/runtime/memory/slates/YYYYMMDD.slate.txt` asynchronously between stub→impl commits, leaving the tree dirty. When proof_emitter checks `git status --porcelain | wc -l`, it detects the dirt and refuses to emit (correctly fail-safe). The workaround (manual parent_ref) is a dev overhead that accumulates.

**Shape:** either (1) batch the slate writes so they land atomically AFTER proof commits close out (late-binding), or (2) wrap proof_emitter calls in a gate that temporarily pauses the slate writer during proofs. Option 1 is simpler; option 2 is tighter if the slate writer is a daemon.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py (where the dirt is detected), devlab/claudecode/slate_sync.py or similar (the writer)

**Design rules:** none apply (infrastructure/ergonomic issue)

**Scope boundary:** fix the dirt/timing issue ONLY; do NOT change proof_emitter's fail-safe (it should always refuse to emit with uncommitted work). In scope: defer writes, or gate them. Out of scope: weakening the clean-tree check, or having proof_emitter auto-amend on dirty.

**Test plan:** during a stub→impl sprint pair (e.g., a proof node that runs twice), assert that proof_emitter succeeds on both runs WITHOUT requiring `parent_ref=` override. A passing test here means the tree stayed clean across all commits (stub + impl + proof commits) without manual intervention.
